An Individual profile must be complete before the platform allows assignment of:
Assessments,
Programs,
Events.

The platform may show section-level completion status, but the key gating rule shall be based on completion of the mandatory section.

---
When the logged-in user has not completed mandatory profile information, the dashboard shall show a prominent prompt at the top of the dashboard requesting completion.

The Update Profile page shall be a full structured page with multiple sections rather than a small inline edit form.

The page shall include:
mandatory section,
optional enrichment sections,
profile picture upload area,
and explanatory messaging.
The Update Profile page shall show guidance explaining that optional fields are not required but can improve platform outcomes, especially assessments.
The system shall allow users to save the profile progressively, even if optional sections remain incomplete.

The system should show whether the mandatory section is complete and whether the user is eligible for assignments.

---

The Profile entity shall support at minimum:

id
userId
tenantId
profileType
fullName
userType
companyName
addressLine1
addressLine2
city
state
country
postalCode
phone
email

highestDegreeHeld

bio

professionalHeadline
expertiseAreas
certifications
coachingExperienceYears
trainingExperienceYears
industryFocus
languagesSpoken
publicProfileReady

Where the profile type is Company, the schema should additionally support:

companyLegalName
companyDisplayName
companyType
companyLogoUrl
companyDescription
industry
employeeCountRange
primaryContactName

The system shall support fields or computed values indicating:

whether mandatory data is complete,
whether the profile is eligible for assignment,
and what percent of all profile fields have been completed.

---

The platform shall support a stepped profile completion approach.
Mandatory fields must be completed before Individuals can receive assignments.
Optional fields must not block core onboarding.
Name, User Type, Phone, and Email shall be treated as non-editable identity fields in user-managed profile updates.
Profile Picture shall be optional.
Professional profiles shall support additional professional context fields.
Company profiles shall support company-specific basic information and location fields.
The dashboard reminder shall be shown when mandatory completion is incomplete.
The Update Profile page shall include both mandatory and optional sections.
The system shall explicitly encourage optional enrichment by communicating its value for better assessments.

---

The Profile system should be implemented as a role-aware but unified profile model.

That means:
one canonical profile object,
shared base fields across all roles,
role-specific extension fields,
and computed completion-state fields used by the dashboard and assignment workflows.

This approach is preferable to creating unrelated profile models for each role because it improves:
consistency,
validation reuse,
analytics,
extensibility,
and downstream feature integration.

---

This Epic is expected to integrate with:

authentication and identity,
dashboard prompting,
assessments,
programs,
events,
future recruitment workflows,
and future Search Coach functionality.

Profile readiness must be available to assignment-related modules so that they can prevent assignment when mandatory profile completion is missing.

QA should validate the following scenarios:

dashboard reminder appears correctly for incomplete profile,
dashboard reminder does not appear for completed mandatory profile,
clicking reminder opens Update Profile page,
mandatory fields are enforced by role,
non-editable fields remain locked,
optional fields remain editable where appropriate,
profile picture upload works where enabled,
progressive save works,
completion status is calculated correctly,
assignment eligibility changes correctly when mandatory fields become complete,
Individuals with incomplete profile cannot be assigned Assessments, Programs, or Events,
Professional-specific fields appear only for Professionals where applicable,
Company-specific fields appear only for Company profiles where applicable.

---

If too many fields are made mandatory initially, onboarding friction will increase.
If mandatory fields are too few, downstream modules may lack sufficient context.
If role-specific profile fields are not modeled early, future public and recruitment use cases may require rework.
If editability rules are unclear, identity data quality may degrade.
If the dashboard prompt is weak or easy to ignore, profile completion rates may remain low.

---

This Epic is considered complete when:

Profile supports Individuals, Professionals, and Companies.
Mandatory and optional fields are clearly modeled.
Dashboard prompt appears when mandatory profile is incomplete.
Update Profile page supports section-based completion.
Optional profile picture upload is supported.
Identity-controlled fields are non-editable.
Individual assignment gating works for Assessments, Programs, and Events.
Professional-specific and Company-specific profile sections work correctly.
Product, Engineering, and QA approve profile flow and data behavior.

---

This document may be used as an implementation specification for GitHub Copilot or other AI coding tools.

When using this document as a coding reference:
treat this file as the source of truth for Profile terminology and behavior,
implement a stepped profile completion model,
separate mandatory and optional sections clearly,
keep identity-controlled fields non-editable,
gate Individual assignments until mandatory profile completion is true,
support role-aware sections for Individual, Professional, and Company,
and keep the model future-ready for Search Coach and recruitment use cases.

stakeholder circulation,

and Copilot-assisted implementation guidance.

End of Document

This document defines the business and functional requirements for the Activity Assignment capability in StudioVerse.

The purpose of this Epic is to allow eligible logged-in users such as Company and Professional actors to assign or recommend activities to another person using a lightweight search-first workflow based on phone number or email address.

This Epic also supports a self-directed activity journey where a logged-in user can click Register Now or Try Now and assign the activity to himself or herself without entering search details.

This Epic establishes the assignment confirmation rules, wallet validation and coin deduction behavior, assignee lookup flow, self-assignment flow, assigned activity records, and post-login visibility of assigned activities for the recipient.

---

This document explains how StudioVerse can operationalize Programs, Assessments, and Events by allowing platform actors to direct those activities to other people or to themselves in a controlled, trackable, wallet-aware way.

This document defines the workflow, eligibility logic, wallet validation, assignment record creation, recipient discovery, self-registration behavior, and assignment visibility rules for assigned activities.

This document provides the functional requirements needed to implement assignment popups, user lookup by email or phone, validation, self-assignment behavior, wallet checks, transaction creation, and assignee activity visibility.

This document provides the basis for validating search behavior, user lookup, self-assignment, assignment gating, insufficient wallet messaging, wallet deductions, activity creation, and assigned activity display behavior.

This document describes the modal-based assignment interaction, self-assignment action flow, conditional states, messaging, and user feedback needed to make assignment and recommendation flows intuitive and reliable.

---

Once logged in, a Company or Professional should be able to assign or recommend a Program, Assessment, or Event to someone else from within the product.

When the user clicks an Assign or Recommend action, the platform shall open a popup that asks for either a phone number or an email address. The user may provide either one, and the system must validate the input before performing search.

If the assignee already exists in registered users, the system shall display the user’s phone number, email address, first name, and last name and enable the next action button such as Assign or Recommend.

If the user is not found, the platform shall inform the assigner that the user was not found and shall request first name and last name, so that assignment can still proceed in a controlled way as a newly identified or not-yet-registered recipient.

In addition, when a logged-in user clicks Register Now or Try Now on a supported activity, the system shall follow the same assignment flow but assign the activity to the same logged-in user. In this self-assignment flow, the system shall not ask for phone number, email address, first name, or last name because the assignee is already known.

Assignment can only happen if the wallet responsible for funding the assignment has enough coins for the activity credit requirement. If insufficient coins are available, the platform must show an error message such as “Not enough coins, please get more coins.” If enough coins are available, the platform shall show a confirmation that n coins will be reduced for the assignment. For zero-credit activities, assignment may proceed without wallet blocking. Once assignment or recommendation is completed, the corresponding activity record shall be created and the recipient shall later see the activity in My activities after login.

---

StudioVerse is designed as a structured multi-tenant platform where Programs, Assessments, and Events are not only consumed directly, but can also be assigned, recommended, or self-started by platform actors.

This means the platform needs a lightweight but reliable mechanism to:
find a target user,
confirm whether they are already registered,
support controlled assignment,
support self-assignment,
debit the appropriate wallet,
and make that activity visible to the assignee in their own experience.

Because the platform already uses wallet-based coin allocation for entitlement, assignment must also integrate directly with coin availability and wallet transaction tracking.

---

StudioVerse currently needs a formal Activity Assignment capability that allows a Company or Professional to assign or recommend an activity to another user without navigating away into a long workflow.

The platform also needs to support a self-serve model in which a logged-in user can directly click Register Now or Try Now and receive the activity as his or her own assigned activity.

The platform also needs to handle both known and unknown assignees. Some recipients may already exist as registered users, while others may need to be identified through minimal information at the time of assignment.

Without this capability:
activities remain isolated rather than operationally shareable,
companies and professionals cannot direct users into the right journey,
users cannot self-initiate supported activities consistently,
wallet debits cannot be tied to assignment events,
and recipients cannot see a reliable list of activities assigned to them after login.

---

The objectives of this Epic are:

Allow Company and Professional users to assign or recommend activities to others after login.
Support assignment and recommendation flows from Programs, Assessments, and Events.
Support self-assignment through Register Now and Try Now for supported activity types.
Open a popup-based flow when the Assign / Recommend action is clicked.
Allow assignee search by phone number or email address for assignment to others.
Validate search input before lookup.
Display first and last name when a registered user is found.
Allow not-found users to be identified through first name, last name, and whichever contact field (phone or email) was not part of the original search.
Create an Assigned Activity record upon successful assignment.
Enforce wallet balance validation before assignment.
Show insufficient wallet messaging when coins are not enough.
Show confirmation before deducting coins.
Record the wallet transaction linked to the assignment.
Show assigned activities to the assignee after login.
Provide a wallet transaction view link from My Wallet.

---

The following are out of scope for this Epic:

external invitations through SMS or email delivery,
unregistered-user onboarding workflow,
public recommendation feeds,
group or bulk assignment,
advanced assignment analytics,
cancellation and refund flows,
and recommender ranking logic.

This Epic focuses on the core assignment / recommendation / self-registration transaction and visibility flow.

---

This Epic includes the following capabilities:

Assign / Recommend button on supported activity types.
Register Now / Try Now button on supported activity types.
Popup-based assignee search for assignment to others.
Search by email or phone.
Input validation.
Registered-user lookup.
Not-found state with first name / last name collection.
Self-assignment without search inputs.
Wallet coin sufficiency check.
Confirmation prompt before wallet debit.
Assigned Activity record creation.
Wallet transaction creation.
Assigned activity visibility to assignee.
Wallet transaction visibility through My Wallet.

---

This Epic does not include:

third-party messaging integrations,
assignment revocation with coin reversal,
bulk assignment import,
assignment reminder automation,
and multi-step onboarding for new external recipients.

---
An Activity is a platform object that may be assigned, recommended, or self-registered by a user, such as an Assessment, Program, or Event.
An Assigned Activity is the record created when one user directs an activity to another user or to himself / herself through an assignment, recommendation, or self-registration action.
The Assigner is the logged-in Company or Professional user who initiates the assignment or recommendation.
The Assignee is the target user to whom the activity is assigned or recommended.
Self-Assignment is the flow in which a logged-in user clicks Register Now or Try Now and the activity is assigned to the same user.
A Recommendation is a lighter form of directed activity suggestion. In UI terms it may share much of the same flow as assignment, though the downstream status may differ.
The Activity Credit Requirement is the number of coins required to complete the assignment of a given activity.

A Wallet Transaction is the ledger record created when coins are debited due to a successful assignment.

---
A Company user may assign or recommend supported activities to another user after login.
A Professional user may assign or recommend supported activities to another user after login.
A logged-in eligible user may click Register Now or Try Now and assign the activity to himself or herself.
The Assignee is the person who receives the assigned activity and later sees it after login.

The SuperAdmin may rely on this assignment model indirectly through wallet, activity, and reporting layers, but the core assignment action described here is primarily performed by Company and Professional users and self-directed users.

---

The first version of this Epic shall support assignment or recommendation for:

Assessments,
Programs,
Events.

The first version of self-assignment through Register Now or Try Now shall support:
Assessments,
Programs,
Events.

The architecture should allow future extension to other activity types without redesigning the assignment model.

---

Supported activity screens shall expose action buttons such as:
Assign
Recommend
Register Now
Try Now

The exact label may depend on activity type or business context.
When the user clicks Assign or Recommend, the platform shall open a popup / modal for assignee lookup and assignment processing.

When the user clicks Register Now or Try Now, the platform shall initiate the same assignment workflow but with the logged-in user pre-selected as the assignee.

The system may either:
open a simplified confirmation modal, or
directly move to wallet confirmation if no additional input is required.

The system shall not ask for phone number, email address, first name, or last name in self-assignment mode.

---

The popup shall include fields for:
phone number,
email address.

The assigner must provide at least one of these values.

The system shall validate:
phone number format if phone is provided,
email format if email is provided,
and ensure at least one of the two fields is present before search is executed.
When the user clicks Search, the system shall look for an existing registered user matching the provided phone number or email address.

If a user is found, the popup shall:
display first name,
display last name,
identify that the target user is recognized,
and enable the next action button such as Assign or Recommend.

If a user is not found, the popup shall show a message such as:
“User not found. Please provide first name, last name, and the missing contact detail.”

The popup shall then require:
first name,
last name,
and the missing contact field (email if phone was searched, or phone if email was searched).

This allows the flow to proceed with a minimally identified assignee record.

---
When a logged-in user clicks Register Now or Try Now, the system shall initiate self-assignment for that activity.
In self-assignment mode, the assignee shall automatically be the currently logged-in user.

The system shall not ask for:
phone number,
email address,
first name,
last name.

After assignee resolution, self-assignment shall follow the same downstream flow as standard assignment, including:
activity eligibility logic,
wallet sufficiency check,
coin deduction confirmation,
assigned activity creation,
wallet transaction creation,
and visibility in assigned activities.

For first release, self-assignment is required for:
Assessments via Try Now or Register Now as applicable,
Programs via Register Now,
Events via Register Now.

---
Each assignable activity shall define or expose the number of coins required for assignment.
Before assignment is finalized, the system shall check whether the wallet responsible for funding that action has enough available coins for that activity.

If there are not enough coins, the system shall show a message such as:
“Not enough coins, please get more coins.”

In this case:
assignment shall not proceed,
wallet balance shall not change,
no wallet transaction shall be recorded,
and no assigned activity record shall be created.

If enough coins are available, the system shall show a confirmation stating that n coins will be reduced from the wallet for the assignment. If the activity requires zero coins, the system may proceed without a wallet-debit warning.

Upon confirmation and successful assignment:
the coins shall be debited,
a wallet transaction shall be created,
and the assigned activity record shall be saved.

---

For this Epic, the wallet check and deduction must be applied against the wallet that business rules define as responsible for funding the assignment.

The default recommendation for first implementation is:
deduct coins from the assigner-owned wallet when that assigner is directly funding the activity assignment,
and deduct coins from the logged-in user wallet during self-assignment through Register Now or Try Now.

If business rules instead require deduction from the assignee wallet in assign-to-other scenarios, that rule must be modeled explicitly and consistently across wallet and assignment services.

Implementation must not leave wallet ownership ambiguous.

---

On successful assignment, recommendation, or self-registration, the system shall create an Assigned Activity record. Recommendations shall be persisted with a distinct downstream status such as recommended so they still appear in My activities.

The Assigned Activity entity shall support at minimum:

id
tenantId
activityType
activityId
activityTitle
assignmentMode (assign, recommend, self_register, self_try)
assignerId
assignerType
assignerName
assigneeUserId
assigneeFirstName
assigneeLastName
assigneePhone
assigneeEmail
searchMatchedRegisteredUser
isSelfAssignment
walletId
walletTransactionId
coinsDebited
status
assignedAt
acceptedAt
completedAt

Suggested status values may include:
assigned
recommended
registered
trial_started
viewed
started
completed
cancelled

The exact first-release status set may be simpler but should be future-ready.

---
When the respective user logs in, they shall see activities that were assigned, recommended, or self-registered to them.

The system should provide a dedicated area such as:
Assigned Activities
or assignment-aware listing inside the relevant module.

The platform shall add a link from My Wallet allowing the user to see transactions recorded against the wallet.

---

The assignment popup shall:
be lightweight,
guide the user clearly through search,
expose state transitions cleanly,
and avoid forcing navigation away from the source activity page.
The self-assignment flow through Register Now or Try Now shall be faster and simpler than assignment to another user because the assignee is already known.

The popup or flow shall show different UI states for:
initial entry,
invalid input,
searching,
user found,
user not found,
self-assignment ready state,
insufficient coins,
confirmation,
success.

The system shall communicate clearly:
whether the user was found,
whether additional name data is needed,
whether the activity is being assigned to self,
whether wallet balance is insufficient,
how many coins will be reduced,
and whether assignment succeeded.

---

The popup shall support:
phone
email
firstName (required only if user not found)
lastName (required only if user not found)

These fields shall not be required in self-assignment mode.

Assignment records should support references to:
wallet transaction,
source activity,
assigner,
assignee,
tenant,
and assignment outcome.

The wallet transaction created for assignment should include:
transaction type,
debit amount,
source activity type,
source activity id,
assignment id,
actor responsible,
and timestamp.

---

Company and Professional users may assign or recommend supported activities after login.
Logged-in users may self-assign supported activities through Register Now or Try Now.
The assigner must provide at least one of phone number or email address before search when assigning to another user.
Search input must be validated before user lookup.
If a registered user is found, the system shall display first and last name and enable assignment action.
If a registered user is not found, the system shall request first name and last name before proceeding.
In self-assignment mode, the system shall not request phone number, email, first name, or last name.
Assignment, recommendation, or self-registration shall create an Assigned Activity record.
Assignment shall proceed only if the relevant wallet has sufficient coins.
If wallet coins are insufficient, assignment shall not happen.
If wallet coins are sufficient, the system shall show a coin deduction confirmation before finalizing.
Successful assignment shall create a wallet transaction.
The recipient shall see assigned activities after login.
My Wallet shall provide access to wallet transaction history.

---

The Activity Assignment system should be implemented as a reusable cross-module service rather than embedding assignment logic separately inside Program, Assessment, and Event pages.

The recommended architecture is:
one common assignment modal,
one shared assignee lookup service,
one shared self-assignment flow,
one shared wallet validation and debit flow,
one canonical Assigned Activity entity,
and activity-type-specific adapters for Programs, Assessments, and Events.

This approach improves:
consistency,
maintainability,
auditability,
reuse,
and future extension to more activity types.

---

This Epic is expected to integrate with:

Profile and identity services for user matching and assignee resolution,
Wallet and transaction services for coin validation and debit,
Assessment module,
Program module,
Event module,
Dashboard or recipient landing pages for assigned activity visibility,
and future notification services.

Assignment services must also respect profile readiness and role logic already established in related platform epics.

---

sectionTitle: Company or Professional Can Open Assignment Popup
text:
As a Company or Professional user, 
I want to click Assign or Recommend on an activity, 
so that I can direct that activity to another person.

Acceptance Criteria
Assign / Recommend action is visible on supported activities.
Clicking the action opens the assignment popup.

---

sectionTitle: User Can Search Assignee by Phone or Email
text:
As a logged-in assigner, 
I want to search by phone number or email address, 
so that I can quickly identify the assignee.

Acceptance Criteria
Popup accepts phone or email.
At least one of them is required.
Input is validated before search.

---

sectionTitle: System Shows Registered User When Found
text:
As a logged-in assigner, 
I want to see the assignee’s first and last name when found, 
so that I can confirm I am assigning to the correct person.

Acceptance Criteria
Matching registered user is displayed.
Action button becomes enabled.

---

As a logged-in assigner, 
I want the system to let me continue when user is not found, 
so that assignment is still possible with minimal identification.

Acceptance Criteria
User-not-found message is shown.
First name and last name fields become required.

---

sectionTitle: Logged-in User Can Self-Assign an Activity
text:
As a logged-in user, 
I want to click Register Now or Try Now, 
so that the activity gets assigned to myself without searching for my own identity.

Acceptance Criteria
Clicking Register Now or Try Now initiates self-assignment.
The system does not ask for phone, email, first name, or last name.
The logged-in user is automatically treated as the assignee.

---

As a Product Owner, 
I want assignment to require enough wallet coins, 
so that activity assignment remains entitlement-controlled.

Acceptance Criteria
Wallet sufficiency is checked before assignment.
Insufficient wallet blocks assignment.
No transaction is created if assignment fails.

---

sectionTitle: Successful Assignment Creates Assignment and Wallet Records
text:
As a Product Owner, 
I want successful assignment to create all required records, 
so that the system remains auditable.

Acceptance Criteria
Assigned Activity record is created.
Wallet transaction is created.
Coin debit amount is stored.
Assignment is linked to the relevant activity.

---

sectionTitle: Assignee Can See Assigned Activities After Login
text:
As a recipient user, 
I want to see activities assigned to me, 
so that I can act on them after logging in.

Acceptance Criteria
Assigned activities are visible after login.
Correct activities appear for the correct assignee.

---

QA should validate the following scenarios:

Assign / Recommend button appears for supported activity types,
Register Now / Try Now appears for supported activity types,
popup opens correctly,
phone validation works,
email validation works,
search requires at least one field,
registered-user lookup returns correct user,
found-state enables action,
not-found state requires first and last name,
self-assignment does not request identity input,
self-assignment resolves logged-in user correctly,
insufficient wallet blocks assignment,
sufficient wallet shows confirmation,
assignment success creates Assigned Activity record,
assignment success creates wallet transaction,
coin amount is correct,
assignee sees assigned activity after login,
My Wallet transaction link displays relevant wallet history,
and cross-module assignment behavior remains consistent across Programs, Assessments, and Events.

---

If wallet ownership rules are ambiguous, incorrect wallet debits may occur.
If user lookup matching is weak, assignment could target the wrong user.
If not-found assignees are not modeled carefully, duplicate user identities may increase later.
If self-assignment is implemented separately from common assignment logic, behavior may drift across modules.
If assignment logic is duplicated separately across activities, maintenance cost will rise.
If wallet debit and assignment creation are not transaction-safe, data inconsistency can occur.

---

This Epic is considered complete when:

Company and Professional users can assign or recommend supported activities.
Logged-in users can self-assign supported activities through Register Now and Try Now.
Popup-based search works using phone or email for assign-to-other scenarios.
Found and not-found user states work correctly.
Self-assignment skips identity input correctly.
Wallet sufficiency validation is enforced.
Confirmation appears before coin deduction.
Successful assignment creates Assigned Activity and wallet transaction records.
Assignees can see assigned activities after login.
My Wallet provides transaction visibility.
Product, Engineering, and QA approve the workflow and data behavior.

---

This document may be used as an implementation specification for GitHub Copilot or other AI coding tools.

When using this document as a coding reference:
treat this file as the source of truth for Activity Assignment terminology and behavior,
build one reusable assignment engine instead of separate flows for each activity type,
validate assignee using phone or email for assign-to-other flows,
handle found and not-found recipients explicitly,
support self-assignment through Register Now and Try Now without asking for identity input,
integrate wallet sufficiency checks before assignment completion,
create both Assigned Activity and Wallet Transaction records on success,
and expose assigned activities clearly to the assignee after login.

---

stakeholder circulation,
backlog refinement,
engineering breakdown,
QA test design,
assignment workflow implementation,
wallet integration planning,
self-registration flow planning,
and Copilot-assisted implementation guidance.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
This document defines the business and functional requirements for the Access Control capability in StudioVerse.

The purpose of this Epic is to ensure that each logged-in user sees only the menus, pages, and high-level actions that are permitted for that user’s role, based on the role matrix defined for Super Admin, Company, Professional, and Individual users.

This Epic also establishes the role-based navigation model, page-level authorization rules, menu rendering behavior, denial handling, and extensibility for future permission refinement.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: This document explains how StudioVerse will restrict access so each user role can only see and use the parts of the platform relevant to their responsibilities.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: This document defines the role-to-menu mapping, visibility rules, action restrictions, and permission boundaries that must be enforced consistently across the product.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: This document provides the functional requirements needed to implement role-based menu rendering, route guards, page authorization, and restricted-action enforcement.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: This document provides the basis for validating correct menu visibility, unauthorized access blocking, role restrictions, and navigation behavior for each user type.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
This document describes how menus should appear by role and how inaccessible features should be hidden or blocked consistently in the interface.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
StudioVerse supports multiple user roles with clearly different responsibilities, so the platform must not show the same menus and capabilities to every logged-in user.

Based on the revised access matrix, Super Admin has the broadest administrative access, Company has organization-scoped management access, Professional has a more limited coach-scoped operational access, and Individual has a restricted self-service access model.

This Epic defines which menus are visible to which roles, what each menu means operationally, and how the system must enforce access both in navigation and in backend authorization checks.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
StudioVerse is a multi-role platform spanning tenant administration, professional services, individual participation, programs, assessments, events, wallets, assigned activities, and activity visibility.

Because these roles are intentionally different, the platform needs explicit role-based access control so users cannot access features outside their responsibility area, such as tenant management, platform-wide user management, or cross-role creation workflows.

This is especially important for menu-driven applications, where incorrect visibility can create confusion, security issues, and accidental access to privileged operations.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
StudioVerse currently needs a formal Access Control capability that determines which menus and pages each user role can access after login.

The platform must align the visible navigation to the approved role matrix and also prevent unauthorized access if a user attempts to open a page directly by URL or indirect navigation.

Without this capability:
users may see irrelevant or unauthorized menus,
role boundaries may become inconsistent across modules,
privileged actions may be exposed incorrectly,
and product behavior may differ across frontend and backend implementations.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
The objectives of this Epic are:

Define a canonical access model for Super Admin, Company, Professional, and Individual users.
Ensure menu visibility is role-based and aligned with the revised access table.
Enforce page-level authorization in addition to menu visibility.
Restrict module actions according to role scope.
Prevent unauthorized access through direct URLs or backend requests.
Keep the authorization model extensible for future fine-grained permissions.
Ensure access behavior is consistent across web UI, APIs, and future modules.
Support stakeholder clarity by documenting each menu’s intended role scope.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
The following are out of scope for this Epic:

field-level permissions inside forms,
advanced policy builders,
delegated admin permission editing,
attribute-based access control,
audit dashboards for permission changes,
and SSO / identity provider permission federation.

This Epic focuses on role-based menu and page access control.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
This Epic includes the following capabilities:

Role-based menu visibility.
Role-based page / route authorization.
Unauthorized access denial.
Role-aware dashboard access.
Module-level access restrictions.
Action-level restrictions for key modules.
Menu configuration aligned to the approved access matrix.
Visibility of My activities for the permitted roles.
Extensible permission structure for future refinement.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
This Epic does not include:

per-user custom permissions,
temporary permission overrides,
approval workflows for access elevation,
and tenant-configurable permission editing.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: Access Control is the set of rules that determines which menus, pages, and actions a logged-in user may see or use.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: A Role is the primary access identity of a user in StudioVerse, such as Super Admin, Company, Professional, or Individual.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: Menu Authorization is the rule that determines whether a menu item is visible in the application navigation for a given role.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: Page Authorization is the rule that determines whether a user may open a given page or route even if they try to access it directly.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: Action Authorization is the rule that determines whether a user can perform a specific action inside an accessible module, such as creating, assigning, promoting, managing, or viewing records.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
Scope Restriction defines the boundaries within which a role can manage data, such as:
all platform users,
only users in a company,
only individuals assigned to a professional,
or only the logged-in user’s own records.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
My activities is the user-facing area where a logged-in user can see activities assigned or relevant to himself / herself based on role and platform behavior.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: The Super Admin has the broadest platform access and can manage platform-wide entities including all users, tenants, assessments, coins, programs, events, and activity assignment.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: A Company user has organization-level access limited to company-relevant modules such as company-scoped users, wallet management, programs, events, cohorts, individuals, assigned activity operations, and activity visibility.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: A Professional user has limited operational access focused on managed individuals, self-wallet, self-created programs and events, self cohorting, activity assignment, and activity visibility.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
An Individual user has a self-service access model focused on dashboard, profile, wallet, self-assignment capability, and personal activity visibility.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text: The system shall render menus based on the logged-in user’s role and only show menu items permitted for that role.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
The system shall enforce authorization at both:
frontend navigation level,
backend page / API level.

Hiding a menu alone shall not be treated as sufficient security.

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
The meaning of each role must remain consistent across all modules so that, for example, a Professional cannot receive broader user-management rights in one module than in another.

---

epicTitle: Access Control and Role-Based Menu Authorization
text:
The following matrix is derived from the revised table and should be treated as the source-of-truth role menu model for this Epic.

| Menu Action | Super Admin | Company | Professional | Individual |
|---|---|---|---|---|

This document defines the business and functional requirements for the Cohort Management capability in StudioVerse.

The purpose of this Epic is to allow Company and Professional users to create and manage cohorts made up of one Professional and multiple Individuals.

This Epic also defines how cohorts are populated, how new Individuals can be created during cohort setup, how cohort activation depends on Professional assignment, and how activities can be assigned to an entire cohort with wallet coin deduction based on cohort size.

---
This document explains how StudioVerse can operationalize group-based delivery by allowing Companies and Professionals to create cohorts and act on them as a unit.
This document defines cohort composition rules, creation flows, activation logic, search-and-add behavior, inline individual creation, and bulk activity assignment logic.

This document provides the functional requirements needed to implement cohort creation, member search, inline individual creation, cohort status behavior, professional association, and cohort-based assignment with wallet deduction.

This document provides the basis for validating cohort composition, search behavior, not-found individual creation, activation rules, edit flows, professional linkage, and bulk assignment calculations.

This document describes the expected Cohort Management experience including cohort creation forms, member lookup, conditional states, and status visibility.

---

StudioVerse needs a Cohort Management capability so that Companies and Professionals can organize multiple Individuals under a named cohort and use that cohort as an operational unit.

A cohort shall always contain:
one Professional,
and more than one Individual.

When a Company creates a cohort, it provides a cohort name and can search existing Individuals by phone number or email to add them to the cohort. If no matching Individual is found, the Company may add a new Individual inline by providing first name, last name, phone number, and email address. These new Individuals are created when the cohort is saved and are then added to that cohort.

A Company may create a cohort without initially assigning a Professional, but the cohort shall become Active only when a Professional is assigned. The Company may later edit the cohort and assign a Professional, at which point all Individuals in that cohort also become associated with that Professional.

When a Professional creates a cohort, the Professional is automatically assigned to the cohort and automatically associated with all Individuals in that cohort.

When assigning activities, the Company or Professional may assign an activity to the whole cohort. In that case, the activity is assigned to every Individual in the cohort, and the total coins deducted shall equal:

\[
\text{Total Coins Deducted} = \text{Number of Individuals in Cohort} \times \text{Cost of Activity}
\]

The wallet deduction must therefore scale by cohort size.

---

StudioVerse already supports user roles such as Company, Professional, and Individual, and it also supports assignment of activities such as Programs, Assessments, and Events.

However, operational delivery often happens in groups rather than one person at a time. A cohort provides that group structure and becomes the unit through which a Company or Professional can manage Individuals collectively.

This capability is also important because it reduces repetitive assignment effort, supports group-based delivery models, and aligns naturally with coaching, training, and cohort-driven learning experiences.

---

StudioVerse currently needs a formal Cohort Management capability that allows a Company or Professional to create and manage a grouped set of Individuals under a shared cohort identity.

The platform also needs to support both:
adding existing Individuals to a cohort,
and creating new Individuals inline when they do not already exist.

Without this capability:
group-based delivery remains manual,
activity assignment becomes repetitive and inefficient,
professional-to-individual linkage may remain fragmented,
and there is no structured operational unit for cohort-based journeys.

The platform therefore needs a role-aware cohort model with clear composition, activation, editing, and assignment behavior.

---

The objectives of this Epic are:

Allow Company to create cohorts.
Allow Professional to create cohorts.
Require every cohort to support one Professional and more than one Individual.
Allow Company to search existing Individuals by phone number or email.
Allow Company to create a new Individual inline if not found.
Save newly entered Individuals when the cohort is saved.
Allow Company to create a cohort with or without immediate Professional assignment.
Mark a cohort Active only when a Professional is assigned.
Allow Company to edit a cohort later and assign a Professional.
Associate all cohort Individuals to the Professional once a Professional is assigned.
Automatically associate the logged-in Professional to the cohort when created by Professional.
Automatically associate all cohort Individuals to that Professional when created by Professional.
Allow cohort-level activity assignment.
Deduct coins based on number of Individuals multiplied by activity cost.
Keep cohort logic aligned with Manage Users and Activity Assignment.

---

The following are out of scope for this Epic:

cohort attendance tracking,
cohort scheduling calendar,
cohort discussion boards,
cohort learning progress dashboards,
bulk import of cohort members,
and cohort-level analytics beyond assignment counts.

This Epic focuses on cohort definition, composition, activation, and group assignment behavior.

---

This Epic includes the following capabilities:

Create Cohort by Company.
Create Cohort by Professional.
Cohort naming.
Search Individuals by phone or email.
Add existing Individuals to cohort.
Add new Individuals inline when not found.
Save new Individuals during cohort creation.
Professional assignment to cohort.
Cohort activation status.
Edit Cohort.
Associate Individuals to Professional on assignment.
Assign activity to whole cohort.
Coin deduction based on cohort member count.
Cohort member listing and management.

---

This Epic does not include:

attendance management,
batch enrollment via spreadsheet,
automated notifications,
session-level scheduling,
and advanced cohort reporting.

---
A Cohort is a named group structure that contains one Professional and multiple Individuals.
An Active Cohort is a cohort that has a Professional assigned and is therefore operationally ready for downstream actions such as guided delivery and assignment.
A Draft or Inactive Cohort is a cohort that may contain Individuals but does not yet have a Professional assigned.

Assign to Cohort is the action through which an activity is assigned to all Individuals within a cohort.

---

A Company user can:
create cohorts,
search and add Individuals,
create new Individuals inline,
optionally assign a Professional,
edit cohorts later,
and assign activities to cohorts.

A Professional user can:
create cohorts,
add Individuals to cohorts,
automatically become the Professional for the cohort,
and assign activities to cohorts.

A cohort shall always conceptually consist of:
one Professional,
and more than one Individual.
A cohort created by Company may initially exist without a Professional, but it shall not be marked Active until a Professional is assigned.
A cohort must contain more than one Individual to meet the expected group definition.

When a Professional creates a cohort, that Professional automatically becomes the cohort Professional.

---

When Company creates a cohort, the user shall provide:
Cohort Name — mandatory

The Company may also:
search existing Individuals by phone number or email,
add matched Individuals to the cohort,
and create new Individuals inline if not found.

If a matching Individual is found:
the system shall display the matching person,
and allow the Company to add that Individual to the cohort.

If no Individual is found:
the system shall ask whether the user wants to add a new Individual,
and if yes, the system shall request:
 - first name,
 - last name,
 - phone number,
 - email address.

When the cohort is saved:
newly entered Individuals shall be created,
all selected and newly created Individuals shall be associated to the cohort.

During Company cohort creation:
Professional assignment may be optional,
the cohort may be saved without a Professional,
but the cohort shall remain inactive until a Professional is assigned.

---

When a Professional creates a cohort, the user shall provide:
Cohort Name — mandatory

The Professional may also:
add Individuals to the cohort using the supported member-add flow.

When a Professional creates a cohort:
the logged-in Professional shall automatically become the cohort Professional.
All Individuals added to a Professional-created cohort shall automatically become associated with that Professional.

Because the Professional is already assigned in this creation flow, the cohort can be active once it satisfies the required Individual membership rules.

---

A Company user shall be able to edit an existing cohort to:
add or remove Individuals,
assign or change the Professional,
and update cohort details such as name.

If a Company-created cohort was saved without a Professional:
Company may later edit the cohort,
assign a Professional,
and upon save, all Individuals in that cohort shall become associated with that Professional.

When a Professional is assigned to a previously inactive cohort:
the cohort status shall become Active if all other validity conditions are satisfied.

---
If an Individual is not found during search, the user may create the Individual inline as part of the cohort creation flow.

The inline new-Individual form shall require:
First Name
Last Name
Phone Number
Email Address
The new Individual record shall be created only when the cohort is saved successfully.

Any new Individual created during Company cohort setup shall inherit the Company tenant. 
Any new Individual created during Professional cohort setup shall inherit the Professional tenant and become associated with that Professional.

---

A cohort may remain draft or inactive when:
it has not yet satisfied minimum structure,
or it does not yet have a Professional assigned.

A cohort shall be marked active only when:
the cohort has a Professional assigned,
and the cohort meets the minimum expected Individual criteria.

---

The platform should support assigning the following to a cohort:
Assessments
Programs
Events

When Company or Professional assigns an activity to a cohort:
the activity shall be assigned to each Individual in the cohort,
each assignment shall behave like an individual assigned activity,
and assignment records should remain traceable back to the cohort assignment event.

The total coin deduction for cohort assignment shall be:

\[
\text{Total Coins Deducted} = \text{Number of Individuals in Cohort} \times \text{Activity Coin Cost}
\]

If:
a cohort has 12 Individuals,
and the assigned Assessment costs 5 coins,

then total coins deducted shall be:

\[
12 \times 5 = 60
\]
Cohort assignment shall proceed only if the relevant wallet has enough coins for the full cohort assignment cost.

If sufficient coins are not available:
cohort assignment shall not proceed,
no assignments shall be created,
and no wallet transaction shall be recorded.

If sufficient coins are available:
the system shall confirm the total deduction,
create assignments for all Individuals in the cohort,
and create the necessary wallet transaction records.

---

The cohort creation experience shall be structured and easy to use, allowing users to:
name the cohort,
search and add Individuals,
create missing Individuals inline,
assign a Professional where applicable,
and save with clear status feedback.

Search by phone or email shall be quick and clearly indicate:
whether a match was found,
whether the result can be added,
and what to do when no match exists.
When no matching Individual is found, the interface shall clearly ask whether the user wants to add a new Individual.

When assigning an activity to a cohort, the UI shall show:
cohort name,
number of Individuals,
activity cost per person,
total coins required,
and confirmation before assignment.

---

The Cohort entity shall support at minimum:

id
tenantId
companyId
name
professionalId (nullable for company-created inactive cohort)
status
memberCount
createdByUserId
createdByRole
createdAt
updatedAt

The Cohort Member model shall support at minimum:

id
cohortId
individualUserId
addedByUserId
addedAt

The system should support linking:
cohort assignment event,
source activity,
member-level assignments,
wallet transaction,
and total coins deducted.

---

Company can create cohorts.
Professional can create cohorts.
A cohort shall support one Professional and more than one Individual.
Company may create a cohort without immediate Professional assignment.
A Company-created cohort shall become active only when a Professional is assigned.
Company can search Individuals by phone or email during cohort creation.
If no Individual is found, the user may add a new Individual inline.
New Individuals are created when the cohort is saved.
All selected or newly created Individuals are assigned to the cohort on save.
When Company later assigns a Professional to a cohort, all cohort Individuals become associated to that Professional.
When Professional creates a cohort, the Professional is automatically assigned to the cohort.
When Professional creates a cohort, all cohort Individuals are automatically associated to that Professional.
Company and Professional can assign activities to the whole cohort.
Total cohort assignment coins deducted equal member count multiplied by activity cost.
Cohort assignment must fail fully if wallet balance is insufficient for the full amount.

---

Cohort Management should be implemented as a reusable cohort domain service rather than embedding cohort logic separately inside Programs, Events, or user lists.

The recommended architecture is:
one canonical Cohort entity,
one Cohort Member relationship model,
one role-aware cohort creation service,
one shared individual lookup and inline-create flow,
one cohort activation rules engine,
and one cohort assignment orchestration service.

This approach improves:
consistency,
maintainability,
reuse,
auditability,
and future extensibility.

---

This Epic is expected to integrate with:

Manage Users for Individual creation and association,
role-based access control,
activity assignment,
wallet and transaction logic,
profile and professional association rules,
and downstream Program, Assessment, and Event flows.

Cohort creation and cohort assignment must remain aligned with the broader Company–Professional–Individual relationship model in StudioVerse.

---

As a Company user, 
I want to create a named cohort, 
so that I can manage a group of Individuals together.

Acceptance Criteria
Company can provide a cohort name.
Company can save a cohort with selected Individuals.
Company can optionally assign a Professional.

---

sectionTitle: Company Can Add Existing Individuals to Cohort
text:
As a Company user, 
I want to search Individuals by phone or email, 
so that I can add existing users to the cohort.

Acceptance Criteria
Search supports phone number or email.
Matching Individuals can be added to cohort.

---

sectionTitle: Company Can Add New Individual When Not Found
text:
As a Company user, 
I want to add a new Individual if search does not find one, 
so that I can continue cohort setup without leaving the flow.

Acceptance Criteria
Not-found state asks if user wants to add a new Individual.
First name, last name, phone, and email are required.
New Individual is created when cohort is saved.

---

sectionTitle: Cohort Becomes Active Only with Professional
text:
As a Product Owner, 
I want a cohort to become active only when a Professional is assigned, 
so that the cohort is operationally complete.

Acceptance Criteria
Company can save cohort without Professional.
Such cohort is not active.
Adding a Professional later activates the cohort if validity conditions are met.

---

As a Professional user, 
I want to create a cohort where I am automatically assigned, 
so that I can manage my own group directly.

Acceptance Criteria
Professional can provide cohort name.
Logged-in Professional becomes cohort Professional automatically.
Added Individuals become associated with that Professional.

---

sectionTitle: Activity Can Be Assigned to Whole Cohort
text:
As a Company or Professional user, 
I want to assign an activity to an entire cohort, 
so that I do not need to assign it person by person.

Acceptance Criteria
Supported activity can be assigned to cohort.
Each Individual receives the assigned activity.
Total coins deducted equal number of Individuals multiplied by activity cost.

---

QA should validate the following scenarios:

Company can create a cohort,
Professional can create a cohort,
cohort name is required,
Individual search works by phone,
Individual search works by email,
found Individuals can be added,
not-found Individuals can be added inline,
new Individuals are created only when cohort is saved,
Company-created cohort can exist without Professional but remains inactive,
assigning a Professional later activates the cohort when valid,
all Individuals become associated with the assigned Professional on later assignment,
Professional-created cohort auto-associates Professional correctly,
Individuals in Professional-created cohort are linked to that Professional,
cohort assignment to activity creates member-level assignments,
coin deduction is correctly calculated,
insufficient wallet blocks the full cohort assignment,
no partial cohort assignment occurs when wallet is insufficient,
and resulting assigned activities appear correctly for each Individual.

---

If member count rules are not enforced properly, cohorts may not match the intended group model.
If inline-created Individuals are saved incorrectly, duplicates may increase.
If later Professional assignment does not backfill Individual association correctly, downstream journeys may break.
If cohort assignment is not transaction-safe, some members may receive activities while others do not.
If wallet deduction is not calculated from final member count correctly, billing and entitlement errors may occur.

---

This Epic is considered complete when:

Company can create and edit cohorts,
Professional can create cohorts,
Individual search and inline creation work,
cohort activation rules work correctly,
Professional back-association works correctly,
cohort activity assignment works for supported activity types,
wallet deduction matches cohort size multiplied by activity cost,
and Product, Engineering, and QA approve the behavior.

---

This document may be used as an implementation specification for GitHub Copilot or other AI coding tools.

When using this document as a coding reference:
treat this file as the source of truth for cohort terminology and behavior,
implement Cohort as a first-class entity,
support search-and-add plus inline Individual creation,
allow Company-created inactive cohorts without Professional,
auto-link Professional-created cohorts to the logged-in Professional,
backfill Individual-to-Professional association when Professional is assigned later,
and use transactional logic for wallet deduction and cohort-wide assignment.

---

stakeholder circulation,

engineering breakdown,

cohort management implementation,
and Copilot-assisted implementation guidance.

When a Company creates a Professional or Individual, the tenant must be inherited automatically from the Company, so tenant selection must not be shown on the form. When a Company creates an Individual, a Coach dropdown should appear as an optional field, populated with existing Professionals of that Company. When a Professional creates an Individual, the Professional must be automatically associated to that Individual without asking the user to select a coach.

epicTitle: Manage Users for Company and Professional
text:
StudioVerse is a multi-tenant platform in which Companies act as tenant-level entities and Professionals operate within those Companies, while Individuals participate in programs, assessments, and related journeys.

Because user onboarding often happens within the Company or coach relationship rather than centrally through Super Admin, the platform needs delegated user creation that respects role scope and tenant association automatically.

This creates a more scalable operating model while preserving control, consistency, and downstream linkage to cohorts, assigned activities, and other platform modules.

---

epicTitle: Manage Users for Company and Professional
text:
StudioVerse currently needs a formal Manage Users capability for Company and Professional roles so these users can onboard the users that sit within their operational scope.

Without this capability:
Companies must rely on Super Admin for all user onboarding,
Professionals cannot directly onboard their Individuals,
tenant and coach associations may become manual or inconsistent,
and user creation becomes slower than needed for real operational use.

The platform therefore needs a role-aware delegated user creation flow with clear rules for what each role can create and how the new user is associated.

This document defines the business and functional requirements for the Referrals capability in StudioVerse.

The purpose of this Epic is to allow Company, Professional, and Individual users to refer new people through a referral link, track those referrals in a separate table, reward the referrer with coins, and support Super Admin-level visibility and reminder actions.

This Epic also defines the menu entry, the referral creation flow, referral status tracking, wallet updates, and reminder mail placeholders.

---
This document explains how referrals can drive growth by allowing users to invite new people into the platform and earn coin rewards for successful joins.
This document defines referral creation, tracking, role-based access, reward logic, and Super Admin oversight.
This document provides the functional requirements needed to implement referral capture, status updates, wallet transactions, admin filtering, and reminder placeholders.
This document provides the basis for validating referral creation, reward issuance, status changes, filtering, and reminder actions.

This document describes the expected navigation, filter controls, referral listing behavior, and creation flow.

---

StudioVerse needs a Referrals capability so that users can invite new Coaches or Individuals through a referral link and receive coin rewards when those referrals are created and later join the platform.

A new menu item called References shall appear in the Manage section. Clicking it shall open the Manage Referrals page.

On this page, users shall be able to view referrals they created, filter by referred type using a radio selector, and create new referrals by selecting whether they are referring a Coach or an Individual, then entering the referred person’s email address and phone number.

Each referral shall be tracked in a separate referral table. When a referral is created, a placeholder mail action shall be triggered. The referring user shall receive 10 coins at the time of referral creation, and an additional 5 coins when the referred person registers and joins the platform.

If a referred person later registers by themselves and matches an existing referral record, the referral status shall become Joined, and the referrer’s wallet shall be updated accordingly.

Super Admin shall also be able to access this functionality, filter referrals by Company, Professional, or Individual, view referral statuses, and initiate reminder mail for selected referrals through a placeholder action.

---

StudioVerse already uses role-based user models and wallet-based coin logic across other modules.

Referral-based growth introduces an additional acquisition channel where existing users can invite new people into the ecosystem and be rewarded for successful onboarding.

This module is important because it:
increases organic growth,
gives users an incentive to refer others,
creates measurable referral tracking,
and supports Super Admin monitoring of referral performance.

---

StudioVerse currently needs a formal referral system that records who referred whom, how the referral was categorized, whether the referral joined, and how coin rewards should be issued.

Without this capability:
referral invites are not tracked centrally,
reward issuance is not auditable,
joined status is not visible,
and Super Admin cannot manage referral reminders or view referral performance.

The platform therefore needs a structured referral workflow with tracking, rewards, and admin oversight.

---

The objectives of this Epic are:

Add a new References menu under Manage.
Open Manage Referrals from the menu.
Allow Company, Professional, and Individual users to create referrals.
Allow Super Admin to view and manage all referrals.
Support referral type selection: Coach or Individual.
Capture referred person’s email and phone number.
Track referrals in a separate referral table.
Trigger a placeholder mail action when a referral is created.
Issue 10 coins to the referrer when the referral is created.
Issue 5 extra coins when the referred person joins.
Update referral status to Joined when the referred person registers.
Support filtering by role type and status.
Support Super Admin filtering by Company, Professional, and Individual.
Support reminder mail initiation through a placeholder action.

---

The following are out of scope for this Epic:

actual email delivery implementation,
referral code generation,
referral link shortening service,
campaign analytics dashboards,
reward redemption,
expiry rules,
and multi-level referral chains.

This Epic focuses on first-level referral tracking, join status, and reward issuance.

---

This Epic includes the following capabilities:

References menu under Manage.
Manage Referrals page.
Referral creation by allowed user roles.
Coach vs Individual referral type selection.
Referral search and listing.
Referral status tracking.
Referral coin reward at creation.
Referral coin reward at join.
Wallet transaction updates.
Super Admin filtering and oversight.
Reminder mail placeholder for selected referrals.

---

This Epic does not include:

referral leaderboard,
multi-tier rewards,
automatic referral code generation,
full email campaign execution,
and fraud detection rules.

---
